Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Database Schema claims adjusters claim_events claim_events has activity codes R A E P N C Ideal Flow R A E P N C DECLARE Model init R existence C responded existence E activities A precedence C activities R noncoexistence E activities C

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Ideal Flow vs Model Ideal R A E P N C Model noncoexistence between E and C This is the biggest red flag The ideal flow has both E and C The model says they cannot coexist This contradicts the business logic completely

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Critical Conflict existence C requires C to be present noncoexistence E C says E and C cannot both be present Result If a trace has E it cannot have C But C must exist Therefore a trace with E is impossible under this model

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the model effectively forces E to never happen This contradicts the ideal flow where E is a mandatory step implied by the sequence

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Other potential issues init R Good precedence C R Redundant if init R and existence C but not contradictory responded existence E A Good for flow but since E is killed by E C conflict this is dormant Missing constraints No constraint for P N A existence No precedence for A before E P before N etc The model is sparse

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypothesis 1 Data error Maybe the extraction algorithm confused Close with Reject or Cancel

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypothesis 3 Process variation Some claims are closed without evaluation for example auto close or fraud detected immediately If so the model might have learned that E and C rarely coexist or the algorithm over generalized

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypothesis 5 Inconsistent updates Policy changed to allow Direct Close for small claims removing E The model was updated to forbid E to enforce this but forgot to remove C from existence

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check 1 Coexistence of E and C Find claims where both E and C exist If these exist the noncoexistence is violated by data

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check 2 Existence of C Find claims without C If many exist existence C is violated

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check 4 Responded Existence E A Find E without prior A

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check 5 Ideal Flow violations Check P N existence Check order R A E P N C
